description_long: This is a rustic, single-story wooden house with a prominent gable roof and a weathered, dark brown exterior. The structure features horizontal wooden planks on the walls and a large, slatted porch area extending from one side, partially covered by a slanted roof. The roof appears slightly damaged or sunken in the middle, revealing some of the interior framework. Windows are asymmetrically placed, with one visible on the front and several on the side, all framed in white. The porch is open with a simple railing, and the main entryway is set back under the porch roof. The overall appearance suggests an older, utilitarian design, possibly suited for rural or semi-urban settings.
description: A weathered, single-story wooden house with a gable roof, asymmetrical windows, and a large, partially covered porch. The structure has a rustic, utilitarian appearance with visible wood planks and a sunken roof section.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gable end, a single window, and the side porch extending to the right.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sunken roof, multiple windows, and the open, slatted porch area.
description_view_2: Rear view with the porch and slatted roof visible, and the back wall mostly solid with no windows.
description_view_3: Top-down view displaying the full gable roof and the rectangular footprint of the house.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entry under a covered porch with simple wooden railings and no decorative elements.
